reviews: Honestly one of the best professors I've had in college. The lectures are well-paced, the examples are relevant, and you can tell they genuinely care whether students actually understand the material. Office hours are worth going to.

This class was way more intense than I expected. The professor clearly knows the material inside out, but sometimes the explanations assume you’re already comfortable with the basics. I had to do a lot of learning on my own to keep up.

The structure of the course is actually really solid. Each topic builds on the previous one, and by the end it all connects. That said, the workload can sneak up on you if you procrastinate even a little.
Not an easy A by any means, but definitely a class where you feel like you earned your grade. The assignments are challenging but fair, and they actually reinforce what’s taught in lectures.
I appreciated how organized everything was. Lecture notes, assignments, and expectations were always clear. It made a difficult subject feel a lot more manageable.
The professor is clearly passionate, but sometimes goes on tangents that make lectures feel longer than they need to be. Still learned a lot overall.
This is one of those classes where you either stay on top of things or fall behind quickly. The professor doesn’t slow down much, but is helpful if you reach out.
Exams were pretty tough but fair. They test understanding rather than memorization, which I actually liked, even if it made studying harder.
I didn’t love the teaching style at first, but it grew on me. By the end of the semester I realized I had learned way more than I thought.
The projects are time-consuming but honestly the best part of the class. They force you to think deeply about the material instead of just skimming it.
Sometimes lectures felt a bit rushed, especially when introducing new concepts. I found myself rewatching recordings a lot.
Great professor if you actually want to learn, not just pass. If you’re looking for an easy class, this is not it.
The grading was consistent and transparent, which I really appreciated. No surprises at the end of the semester.
I struggled in the beginning, but once things clicked, it became one of my favorite classes.
The professor explains complex ideas in a surprisingly intuitive way, but you still need to put in the work outside of class.
Workload is heavy but manageable if you plan ahead. Definitely not something you can cram for.
I wish there were more practice problems provided, especially before exams.
Lectures are engaging and interactive, not just reading off slides.
This class pushed me harder than most, but I came out of it much more confident in the subject.
The professor is approachable and willing to help, but you have to take initiative.
Some assignments felt overly complicated for what they were trying to teach.
Very detail-oriented teaching style, which can be helpful but also overwhelming at times.
I liked how real-world applications were incorporated into lectures.
The pacing is fast, but not unreasonable if you stay consistent.
Definitely one of the more challenging classes I’ve taken so far.
The professor has high expectations, but it feels justified given how much you learn.
I found the lectures clear, but the jump to assignments was sometimes big.
Exams were tricky but fair, with no weird surprises.
You’ll spend a lot of time on this class, but it pays off.
The professor clearly cares about student success, even if the class is tough.
Some lectures felt a bit disorganized, especially toward the end of the semester.
The material is inherently difficult, but the professor does a decent job breaking it down.

I would recommend this professor if you’re serious about the subject.

I liked the balance between theory and practical work.
